comment.body: CCP is like early medieval England in some ways. CCP is run by about 1000 families, they get all the top jobs, they are a bit like feudal lords. The business owners feel like they have created businesses and true wealth but as Jack Ma found out it’s an illusion, you are ultimately answerable to the lords of the CCP. The aristocracy similar to the Norman lords of 12 th century. I wouldn’t stretch the analogy to far but there is some similarities

comment.body: Under normal conditions, yes.  But the action in the long end of the curve (30yr) is very unnatural.
comment.raw_body: Under normal conditions, yes.  But the action in the long end of the curve (30yr) is very unnatural.

comment.body: Natural conditions are when bond prices settle at fair value across the curve under liquid conditions, based on expectations for growth, expectations for inflation, etc.  Unnatural conditions are when your boss and his boss walk into your office and tell you to sell treasuries at whatever price you can get right now.
comment.raw_body: Natural conditions are when bond prices settle at fair value across the curve under liquid conditions, based on expectations for growth, expectations for inflation, etc.  Unnatural conditions are when your boss and his boss walk into your office and tell you to sell treasuries at whatever price you can get right now.

comment.body: What could China do to increase the value of the Yuan? Would that be helpful or hurtful to them at this stage? If they backed it by gold, would that increase the perceived value of their bonds to compete against the US? Or would that mess up their ability to compete production wise? It seems, if they continue to fully embrace automation, that they might be able to get away with some positive currency valuation.

comment.body:
Ya that’s what I figured. So there is no global advantage to having a strong currency. I guess that’s where gold/bitcoin fill the void.

Even if a country did back their currency by gold, who would believe them? Just silly we play this stupid game pretending like this system isn’t contrived by humans. Every fiat currency ends up being manipulated and devalued. Can humanity evolve past this? Why does every country in the world have to devalue in order to create growth? Is this a root issue in capitalism? Or just some baseline law of human nature that cannot be overcome. We have to create more currency constantly for the free market to be able to compete for? As money that is already won stays stagnant, sitting in assets? I’ve always wondered about this.
